prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the sky be blue, eh? Well, ye see, me hearty, 'tis all due to the way the sun's light be scattered by the air molecules in the atmosphere. The shorter wavelengths of light, which be blue, scatter more than the longer wavelengths, which be red. So when ye look up at the sky, ye see more of the blue light that's been scattered all around. 'Tis a bit like when ye shine a lantern through a fog - the light be scattered in all directions, but ye see more of the blue light than the red. So there ye have it, me bucko - the sky be blue 'cause of the way the light be scattered by the air. Now, if ye'll excuse me, I've got some plunderin' to be gettin' on with!
